heading: Administrative provisions
label: 3007 Administrative provisions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Administrator of the Administration for Community Living of the Department of Health and Human Services (referred to in this section as the “Administrator”) shall be responsible for the administration of this chapter.

children.heading: Collaboration
children.label: 2 Collaboration
children.text: The Administrator shall consult with the Office of Special Education Programs of the Department of Education, the Rehabilitation Services Administration of the Department of Education, the Office of Disability Employment Policy of the Department of Labor, and other appropriate Federal entities in the administration of this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: the needs of individuals with all types of disabilities and across the lifespan; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the use of assistive technology in all potential environments, including employment, education, and community living.

children.heading: Funding limitations
children.label: B Funding limitations
children.text: For each fiscal year, not more than ½ of 1 percent of the total funding appropriated for this chapter shall be used by the Administrator to support the administration of this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall assess the extent to which entities that receive grants under this chapter are complying with the applicable requirements of this chapter and achieving measurable goals that are consistent with the requirements of the grant programs under which the entities received the grants.

children.heading: Provision of information
children.label: 2 Provision of information
children.text: To assist the Secretary in carrying out the responsibilities of the Secretary under this section, the Secretary may require States to provide relevant information, including the information required under subsection (d).

children.heading: Corrective action
children.label: 1 Corrective action
children.text: If the Secretary determines that an entity that receives a grant under this chapter fails to substantially comply with the applicable requirements of this chapter, or to make substantial progress toward achieving the measurable goals described in subsection (b)(1) with respect to the grant program, the Secretary shall assist the entity, through technical assistance funded under or other means, within 90 days after such determination, to develop a corrective action plan.section 3005 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Partial or complete termination of funding under the grant program, until the entity develops and complies with such a plan.

children.label: B
children.text: Ineligibility to participate in the grant program in the following fiscal year.

children.label: C
children.text: Reduction in the amount of funding that may be used for indirect costs under for the following fiscal year.section 3003 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: Required redesignation of the lead agency designated under or an entity responsible for administering the grant program.section 3003(c)(1) of this title

children.heading: Appeals procedures
children.label: 3 Appeals procedures
children.text: The Secretary shall establish appeals procedures for entities that are determined to be in noncompliance with the applicable requirements of this chapter, or have not made substantial progress toward achieving the measurable goals described in subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Secretarial action
children.label: 4 Secretarial action
children.text: As part of the annual report required under subsection (d), the Secretary shall describe each such action taken under paragraph (1) or (2) and the outcomes of each such action.

children.heading: Public notification
children.label: 5 Public notification
children.text: Not later than 30 days after taking an action under paragraph (1) or (2), the Secretary shall notify the public, by posting on an easily accessible portion of the internet website of the Department of Health and Human Services, notification of each action taken by the Secretary under paragraph (1) or (2). As a part of such notification, the Secretary shall describe each such action taken under paragraph (1) or (2) and the outcomes of each such action.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than December 31 of each year, the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the President, the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate, and the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives a report on the activities funded under this chapter to improve the access of assistive technology devices and assistive technology services to individuals with disabilities.

children.label: A
children.text: a compilation and summary of the information provided by the States in annual progress reports submitted under ; andsection 3003(f) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a summary of the State applications described in and an analysis of the progress of the States in meeting the measurable goals established in State applications under .section 3003(d) of this titlesection 3003(d)(3)(C) of this title

children.heading: Construction
children.label: e Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to affect the enforcement authority of the Secretary, another Federal officer, or a court under any other applicable law.

children.heading: Effect on other assistance
children.label: f Effect on other assistance
children.text: This chapter may not be construed as authorizing a Federal or State agency to reduce medical or other assistance available, or to alter eligibility for a benefit or service, under any other Federal law.
